text: The surgeon exposes the tumor, which is clearly visible above the surgical instruments. The left-sided superior vena cava (SVC) is also visible, with the phrenic nerve lying over it.
The surgeon uses a Maryland dissector and fenestrated forceps with wristed articulation to precisely dissect tissues, aligning the instruments to access the correct anatomical plane.

The surgeon stands at the patient's side, demonstrating the ability to swiftly switch between wristed instruments and standard VATS instruments, including an energy device visible in the surgical field.
